highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.183: Auto Mode Now Blocks Dangerous Git Commands
highlights.summary: Claude Code's auto mode now prevents destructive git operations like `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, and `git stash drop` unless you explicitly asked to discard local work. It also blocks `git commit --amend` on commits the agent didn't make in the current session, protecting your history from unintended rewrites. This is a significant safety improvement for developers who use auto mode on real codebases.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.181: Set Config Options Directly from the Prompt
highlights.summary: You can now type `/config key=value` in the Claude Code prompt to change settings on the fly — for example, `/config thinking=false` to disable extended thinking without leaving your workflow. This works in interactive mode, headless `-p` mode, and Remote Control, making it much faster to tweak behavior mid-session. Beginners will find this especially handy for experimenting with settings without digging through config files.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.178: Fine-Grained Permission Rules with Tool Parameter Matching
highlights.summary: A new `Tool(param:value)` syntax lets you write permission rules that match specific tool inputs, such as `Agent(model:opus)` to block subagents from using the Opus model. Skills stored in nested `.claude/skills` directories now also load automatically when you're working on files in those directories, with the most local skill winning on name conflicts. This gives teams much more precise control over what Claude is allowed to do in different parts of a monorepo.

highlights.title: Anthropic Pauses Subscription Credit Change for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic announced it is delaying a planned change that would have moved `claude -p`, the Agent SDK, and third-party apps off subscription rate limits onto a separate monthly credit pool. Developers who rely on these features can continue using their existing subscription limits for now, giving them more time to plan for any future billing changes. Keep an eye on official Anthropic emails and the HN thread for updates on the new timeline.

highlights.title: Community Build: Claude Code Native Diff Integration for Visual Studio
highlights.summary: A developer built a Claude Code extension for classic Visual Studio (not VS Code) that brings native diff views with accept/reject buttons, using the same protocol as the official VS Code and JetBrains plugins. If you're working in Visual Studio on .NET or C++ projects, this fills a real gap since there's been no official support. Check the GitHub repo's GIF demo to see the diff workflow in action before installing.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (No Code)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through getting started with Claude Code from scratch in under 20 minutes, requiring no prior coding experience. It's a great first stop if you've just installed Claude Code and want a quick, practical orientation before diving into longer courses. Watch it alongside VS Code open to follow along in real time.

highlights.title: Agent Rigor: Open-Source Tool to Stop Claude Code from Doom-Looping
highlights.summary: Agent Rigor is a community-built tool designed to detect and interrupt runaway AI coding loops where the agent keeps retrying a failing task indefinitely. Doom-looping wastes API credits and can leave your codebase in a partially broken state, so adding a guard like this to your workflow is a practical safety net. It's worth checking out especially if you run Claude Code in headless or automated pipelines.

tips_of_the_day: Before starting a long auto-mode session, make sure your working tree is committed or stashed — Claude Code v2.1.183 now blocks accidental destructive git commands, but a clean baseline means you can always roll back safely if something unexpected happens.

Use `/config thinking=false` at the start of quick, straightforward tasks to reduce latency and token usage; switch it back on with `/config thinking=true` only when you need Claude to reason through a complex problem.

If Claude Code seems to stall during a long task, wait the full 20 seconds before assuming it's stuck — the updated stream-stall hint in v2.1.185 now shows a countdown before retrying, so a visible 'Waiting for API response' message means it's still working and will retry automatically.
